comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
**Location:** Final line of the proof of Theorem 2.6: "properly proximal relative to $\Gamma(\cap_{v\in\mathcal V(\mathcal G)}\mathrm{link}(v))=\mathbb C$".

To conclude full proper proximality from Lemma 2.4, one needs $\bigcap_{v}\mathrm{link}(v)=\varnothing$, i.e. no vertex $w$ lies in $\mathrm{link}(v)$ for **every** $v$.

**Required (graph-theoretic):** prove from irreducibility and $|\mathcal V(\mathcal G)|\ge3$ that no vertex is adjacent to all others. A vertex $w$ adjacent to all others would give the join decomposition $\mathcal V=\{w\}\sqcup(\mathcal V\setminus\{w\})$ with every pair across the partition an edge — contradicting irreducibility (here $|\mathcal V|\ge3$ ensures $\mathcal V\setminus\{w\}$ is nonempty and the partition is nontrivial). Conclude $\bigcap_v\mathrm{link}(v)=\varnothing$. (Note $w\notin\mathrm{link}(w)$, so the diagonal is already excluded; confirm the intersection is genuinely empty.)
